linting.notes: Linting is comprehensive with 24+ enabled linters. The Makefile target 'make lint' downloads golangci-lint to bin/ if not present. CI uses golangci-lint-action for caching.

testing.run_command_expanded: KUBEBUILDER_ASSETS="$(./bin/setup-envtest use 1.32 --bin-dir ./bin -p path)" go test $(go list ./... | grep -v /e2e) -coverprofile cover.out
testing.run_single_file: Not supported - Go tests must be run by package
testing.mock_patterns: Uses envtest to provide fake Kubernetes API server
testing.notes: Unit tests use envtest (controller-runtime's fake K8s API server). E2E tests in test/e2e/ require a Kind cluster and are excluded from 'make test'. Coverage is 76.4% for the controller package. Tests follow Ginkgo BDD style with Describe/It blocks.

ci.notes: Both workflows run on ubuntu-latest with Go version from go.mod (1.23.0). Lint uses golangci-lint v1.63.4. Test runs 'go mod tidy' first to ensure dependencies are tidy, then 'make test'.

build.notes: Build uses Kubebuilder code generation. Dockerfile uses golang:1.23 as builder and distroless as runtime. The 'make build' target runs manifests, generate, fmt, and vet before compiling.

conventions.test_naming: Ginkgo BDD style: Describe(), It(), BeforeSuite(), AfterSuite(). Test functions use underscores: TestMCPServerReconciler_reconcileMCPServerDeployment
conventions.import_style: Grouped imports: standard library, third-party, internal. Uses Ginkgo dot imports (. "github.com/onsi/ginkgo/v2")
conventions.code_generation: Uses controller-gen for CRD/RBAC generation and DeepCopy methods
conventions.notes: This is a Kubebuilder-generated operator following standard Kubernetes operator patterns. Code is organized with api/, internal/controller/, config/, and pkg/ directories.

lint_commands.notes: Downloads golangci-lint v1.63.4 to bin/ on first run, then executes linting. No issues found.
Direct invocation after make lint has downloaded the binary. Clean run.

test_commands.notes: Full test suite passed. Downloads controller-gen, setup-envtest, and K8s binaries on first run. Tests run with envtest providing fake K8s API. Coverage: 76.4% for internal/controller. Output: 'ok  github.com/opendatahub-io/mcp-server-operator/internal/controller 6.277s coverage: 76.4% of statements'

build_command.notes: Builds bin/manager binary. Runs code generation (manifests, generate), fmt, vet before compiling.

container_recipe.notes: The container recipe is straightforward. After 'go mod download', 'make test' handles all setup (downloads tools, generates code, runs tests). Tools are downloaded to ./bin/ and cached. E2E tests (test/e2e/) are excluded from 'make test' and require a Kind cluster.

results.output_snippet: Downloaded golangci-lint v1.63.4, ran linting, no issues found
results.notes: Lint passed with no violations. Downloaded golangci-lint on first run.
Direct lint invocation confirmed clean codebase.
All unit tests passed. Coverage 76.4% in controller package. Envtest binaries downloaded successfully.
Specific test execution via -run flag works.
Build successful, binary created at bin/manager.

validation.summary: All validation steps passed: install ✓, lint ✓, fmt ✓, vet ✓, test ✓ (76.4% coverage), build ✓. Container recipe is production-ready.

gaps: E2E tests (test/e2e/) require a Kind Kubernetes cluster and cannot be run in a simple container
No coverage threshold is enforced - current coverage is 76.4% for controller package
Some packages (api/v1, cmd, test/utils) have 0% test coverage
The Makefile's 'make test-e2e' target expects a Kind cluster to be already running
notes: This is a well-structured Kubernetes operator with comprehensive linting, good test coverage (76.4%), and clean CI configuration. All lint and test commands validated successfully in a golang:1.23 container. An AI agent can clone, patch, lint, and test this repo with a simple container setup. E2E tests require cluster infrastructure (noted in gaps). The Makefile provides clear targets for all operations. Code follows Kubebuilder patterns and uses Ginkgo/Gomega for BDD-style testing.
